chapter_title: Practical Geometry Symmetry & Visualising Solid Shapes
question_text: A net of a 3-D shape is a sort of skeleton - outline in 2-D, which, when folded results in the 3-D shape.
answer_explanation: **   ****Steps of construction:** **Step I:** Draw a line l and mark a point C outside it. **Step II:** Take a point B on line l and join BC. **Step III:** Draw line parallel to line l passing through C. **Step IV:** Mark a point D on line m, at a distance of 2.2 cm from C. **Step V:** Through D draw AD || BC. Line l is parallel to line m Also, AD || BC, AB = DC = 2.2 cm shaalaa.com     Chapter 12: Practical Geometry Symmetry and Visualising Solid Shapes - Exercise  Chapter 12 Practical Geometry Symmetry and Visualising Solid Shapes   Exercise | Q 97. | Page 375 ####  Video TutorialsVIEW ALL  ####

question_text: Draw all the lines of symmetry for the following letters if they exist.
answer_explanation: **   ****Steps of construction:** **Step I:** Draw a line segment BC of length 5.5 cm. **Step II:** Draw an angle of 120° on point B and produce it to ray Y. **Step III:** Draw an angle of 30° on point C and produce it to ray X. **Step IV:** Extend BY and CX to intersect at point A. Hence, ΔABC is the required triangle with BC = 5.5, ∠ABC = 120° and ∠ACB = 30°. shaalaa.com     Chapter 12: Practical Geometry Symmetry and Visualising Solid Shapes - Exercise  Chapter 12 Practical Geometry Symmetry and Visualising Solid Shapes   Exercise | Q 100. | Page 375 ####  Video TutorialsVIEW ALL ") ####

answer_key: Desired views are shown in Fig. 12.10 below
answer_explanation: Nature provides many beautiful examples of symmetry, such as the wings of a butterfly and a peacock or the petals of a flower. Symmetric objects have parts that are congruent. A figure has line symmetry if you can draw a line through it so that the two sides are mirror images of each other. The line is called the line of symmetry.

chapter_title: Practical Geometry Symmetry & Visualising Solid Shapes
question_text: Given a line l and a point M on it draw a perpendicular MP to l where MP = 5.2cm and a line q parallel to l through P. ACCA EE E AD A D AE      15-04-2018 AEAC
answer_explanation: Steps : (i)   Draw a line l . (ii) Take a point M on it. (iii) Draw an angle of 90° at M with l which is perpendicular to l at M. (iv) With M as centre and radius 5.2 cm, draw an arc which intersects the above perpendicular at point P. MP is the required prependicular (v) At P, draw an angle of 90° with PM and produce to make a line q.
